title: Native CatBoost categorical-combination model
summary: The data has high-value categorical structure: Driver, Race, Year, Compound, Stint, and their interactions.
rationale: The data has high-value categorical structure: Driver, Race, Year, Compound, Stint, and their interactions. CatBoost is designed to transform categorical combinations into numeric statistics internally, avoiding brittle manual one-hot encodings.
implementation_hint: Train a standalone CatBoostClassifier with categorical columns preserved as strings/categories, class imbalance handled via scale_pos_weight or auto_class_weights, eval_metric=AUC, early stopping, and several seeds. Include engineered numeric features from the best wrapper but avoid precomputed target encodings in this variant. Blend CatBoost predictions with AutoGluon GBM/XGB by rank or weighted average.
expected_effect: Adds model diversity and may capture Driver-Race-Compound strategy habits better than LightGBM/XGBoost on manually encoded categories.
risk: CatBoost can overfit race identities under random splits, so require GroupKFold by Race/Year sanity checks before trusting leaderboard gains.
